name: July 2025 Dinner
description: Expansive discussion at Ramen Space covering vibe coding, Claude Code vs Cursor, AI eating software, legacy programming languages, evolving tech roles, and startup distribution strategy.

topics.text: Vibe coding and the future of development — Rachel demoed a language learning app built in hours using Lovable
Claude Code vs Cursor debate — most attendees favoured Claude Code's new paradigm; multi-agent approach to building features
AI is eating software — personalised, custom, even throwaway software becomes possible, but distribution and marketing remain key
Legacy programming languages and AI — opportunities to maintain or migrate COBOL, Fortran, q code using AI
Evolving roles of tech professionals — coalescence of developer, PM, and designer roles giving generalists an advantage
AI in the enterprise — most valuable use cases are internal, augmenting employee workflows rather than customer-facing chatbots
Startup insights — distribution obsession over product obsession; founder-led sales crucial in early stages
Recommended reading on AI's future — AI 2027 and Situational Awareness essays
